Run content-level lint checks (titles, alt text, broken local links, future dates, duplicate slugs, malformed frontmatter)

Usage:
  laurel lint [--config <path>] [--json] [--strict] [--max-title-length <n>]

Options:
  -c, --config <path> Config path(s); repeat or comma-separate to deep-merge in order
  -j, --json          Emit findings as JSON ({ count, findings: [{ rule, severity, file, message }] }) for CI consumption
  --strict            Exit with non-zero status if any warning-level findings were emitted (errors always exit non-zero)
  --max-title-length <n>  Override the max title length before a warning is emitted (default: 70 characters; Google SERP cut-off rule of thumb)
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > .laurelrc > config > default).
  Naming: LAUREL_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --config → LAUREL_LINT_CONFIG

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  laurel lint                                  # warn-level summary table
  laurel lint --strict                         # exit non-zero on any warning
  laurel lint --json | jq                      # CI-friendly findings stream
  laurel lint --max-title-length 60
